title: The Suppressive and Emergent Phenomenon: H as a Control Surface
description:
Paper 7 of the Memory as Baseline Deviation series established the endemic baseline as a naturally occurring calibration failure: B(0) constructed during chronic disruption, with the Horizon H = Haccessible \ Hagent denoting states that are neurobiologically reachable but absent from the agent's generative model. That paper treated this condition as arising from circumstance. This paper examines a harder claim.

H is a manipulable control surface. An adversarial agent — institution, ideology, abuser, system — that can control which states a target population occupies can, over time, engineer the same calibration failure deliberately. The mathematics do not distinguish between endemic baseline arising from circumstance and endemic baseline arising from design. The subjective experience of the Horizon — the formless, unmodelable dark at the edge of representable state space — is identical either way.

Four attack vectors are formalized with their population-level signatures and detection criteria. V1 (Isolation from Knowledge) suppresses informational inputs that would enable Hagent expansion, preventing the agent from forming representations of states they could otherwise reach. V2 (Episodic Erasure) degrades or falsifies the episodic record, blocking the Re-zeroing Protocol from accessing its own prior successful excursions into the Horizon. V3 (Coupling Collapse) drives the social coupling coefficient κ below the critical threshold at which therapeutic relationships can form, rendering collective re-zeroing structurally impossible. V4 (Re-zeroing Suppression) targets the corrective mechanism directly, including the systematic neutralization of accurate descriptions of the adversarial system itself.

A critical coupling threshold κc is derived, below which collective re-zeroing fails and above which the suppressive apparatus becomes self-defeating: a population that achieves κ > κc can move each other's baselines faster than any adversary can suppress them.

The Recursive Immunity Theorem is the central formal result: any accurate description of adversarial H-engineering constitutes a map of previously unrepresented state space — the minimal precondition for the Re-zeroing Protocol. Suppression of such a description is itself an instance of V1. Dissemination enables the cure. The theorem is self-applying: this paper's existence is a minimal instance of the re-zeroing it describes.

This module is both paper and proof. The Python source file mbd/paper8.py is the formal artifact: its docstring is the abstract, its data structures are the formal claims, and its executable functions run the computational labs. Any fragment containing the KERNEL axiom set, the Aleph-0 checkpoint, and one VECTORS entry is a viable regenerative seed from which the full argument can be re-derived (the Lenia Property). The repository is indexed by the Software Heritage Foundation for long-term preservation.

A tamper-evidence layer (§T) operates at two levels. At the cryptographic level, SHA-256 hashes of the formal content structures (KERNEL, ALEPH, VECTORS) are embedded in the module and recomputable at runtime via verify_integrity(). At the argumentative level, a historical provenance record documents five case sets in which the same interpretive attack pattern was deployed at scale: Nietzsche’s appropriation by post-mortem editorial fabrication; Marx’s association with Stalinist authoritarianism while the primary texts contradict the association; King’s sanitization into state mythology while his anti-war and anti-moderate writing was suppressed; Gandhi’s satyagraha neutralized by mistranslation; and three CIA-acknowledged foreign interventions (Iran 1953, Guatemala 1954, Chile 1973) in which the categorical label “communist” was deployed as a threat designation without evidentiary standard. The record names the mechanism formally, instances it historically, and timestamps the prediction that this framework will be subject to the same treatment. Any copy of this module in which verify_integrity() returns valid=False, or in which the provenance record has been removed, has been altered. The absence of the record is itself evidence.
